rationale.R1.2: The excerpt chains several technical claims (abstention threshold τ, bootstrap band crossing zero, panel reliability fraction) whose logical consistency is reviewable regardless of delegation intent.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are present (14/19 abstentions, 5 accurate returns, 1-ulp match on regular subset, 35-integral CHEBINT suite) but the supporting experiments are not shown; alignment between these claims and any underlying data is a natural review target.
rationale.R1.4: The paper asserts 'first adaptive quadrature scheme' with principled abstention — a strong novelty claim whose delta vs. Bornemann (2003) reliability-score work and QUADPACK-style safeguards is non-trivial to verify.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'take a look at this paragraph' frequently signals a prose-quality pass; sentence clarity, register consistency, and paragraph flow are all plausible primary targets.
rationale.R1.7: Three citations are invoked (Patterson 1968, Kowalski & Tran 2019, Bornemann 2003); accuracy, appropriate attribution of the reliability-score idea to Bornemann, and coverage of related work are reviewable from the text alone.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary concern writing quality and prose clarity (grammar, flow, word choice), rather than technical or scholarly accuracy (logical consistency, evidence support, citation correctness, novelty claims)?
